theme: Presenter cockpit — the LAST milestone of v1.9. A standalone served panel (rext demo-stack, offset port 7700+N*10000, NOT an in-app overlay — the zero-platform-repo-edit line holds) that reads the SAME stack.stories.yaml that seeded the data and renders each story -> its hero trio with [Login as] + [Jump to section]. The two actions collapse into ONE FAPI handshake redirect (?__clerk_identity=<key>&redirect_url=<jump_to>, M37's seat-switch seam) so a demo-giver picks a hero and lands logged-in on her screen in one move. Ships the roster-export PRODUCER (stackseed --roster-export, single-sourced from the seeder's own id-derivation -> FAKE_FAPI_ROSTER) + the cockpit-manifest exporter (stackseed --cockpit-export, the Go-projected JSON the stdlib Python panel reads so D9's single-source holds without PyYAML) + the O9 deep-link catalog (next-web routes per vantage). Gated on DEMO_STORIES=1; default-off keeps every existing demo byte-identical. Tooling + docs only — zero platform-repo edits.

review_summary: close GREEN — 8 findings, 0 blocking. Scope (1): M38-D7 re-fated Fate-3 -> Fate-1 LAND-NOW (the deferral re-audit + the orchestrator both landed here; recorded as M38-D8). Code-quality (4): [must-fix x2] roster.go:93 + roster_test.go:74 still called the OLD roleForIndex while users.go was on roleForHero — the three-write lockstep was broken (a manager hero would export org_role=member but seed as admin); both switched to roleForHero. [should-fix] gofmt cockpit_test.go (multibyte arrow alignment). [nice-to-have] cockpit.py defensive empty-key skip. Docs (3): stories-spec.md FAPI port-offset (5400+N*10000) made concrete in the handshake example + the vantage-faithful org_role blend (#M38-D8); README.md non-fatal-cockpit + DEMO_NO_COCKPIT cross-ref. Decision-triage: M38-D8 -> blended into stories-spec.md; M38-D1..D6 already blended at build; M38-D7 archived (superseded by D8). Adversarial (Phase 2c): the cockpit URL builders are urlencode-escaped + html.escape'd (no XSS / no broken handshake); the empty-key path was the one defensive gap (now skipped + tested); the cockpit-serve-non-fatal vs roster-fail-loud split is BY DESIGN (D6), not a bug.
